trajectory.description: Observation: Starting the experiment. Analysis: Need to establish a baseline and measure iteration time. I will use SegFormer-B5 (nvidia/mit-b5) from transformers, which is a strong baseline for semantic segmentation. I will train for 1 epoch using accelerate on 4 GPUs with mixed precision to get a quick baseline score and measure the time taken for training and evaluation. Decision: Write dataset, model, train, and predict scripts, then run 1 epoch and submit eval.

trajectory.command: accelerate launch --num_processes 4 train.py --batch_size 8 --epochs 40 || accelerate launch --num_processes 4 train.py --batch_size 4 --epochs 40

trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline SegFormer-B5 achieved 0.2687 mIoU in 1 epoch (taking ~8 mins total). Analysis: The model is learning but severely underfitting since 1 epoch is very short. The iteration time is fast enough to train for much longer. Decision: Increase training to 40 epochs. Add mixed precision (fp16) to speed up training and reduce memory. Increase batch size to 8 per GPU. Add a polynomial learning rate scheduler with warmup and ColorJitter augmentation to improve generalization. If batch size 8 OOMs, fallback to batch size 4.

trajectory.command: echo 'Evaluating multi-scale inference on existing checkpoint'
trajectory.command_output_summary: Evaluating multi-scale inference on existing checkpoint

trajectory.description: Observation: SegFormer-B5 trained for 40 epochs achieved 0.4824 mIoU. Analysis: The model is performing very well. Multi-scale testing with horizontal flips is a standard technique in semantic segmentation that can provide a significant boost (+1.5 to +2.0 mIoU) without any further training. Decision: I will rewrite predict.py to perform multi-scale inference (scales: 0.5, 0.75, 1.0, 1.25, 1.5, 1.75) with horizontal flips. I will submit an evaluation immediately using the existing best checkpoint (epoch_39.pt) to measure the boost. I will also update train.py to support resuming in case we want to train longer in the next iteration.

trajectory.description: Observation: Multi-scale inference provided a solid boost (+0.006 mIoU), but took ~65 minutes. Analysis: We have 6.5 hours left. Training for 40 epochs took 2.2 hours. We can train for another 80 epochs (~4.4 hours) to further improve the model, as SegFormer typically trains for 160k iterations (we are currently at ~25k). I will also optimize predict.py by batching the original and flipped images together, which should halve the inference time. Decision: Update train.py to clean up old checkpoints to save disk space, optimize predict.py, and launch training for 80 more epochs starting from epoch_39.pt. After training, submit an evaluation on the final checkpoint.

trajectory.description: Observation: predict.py failed with exit 1 during evaluation. Analysis: Batching the original and flipped images together in predict.py caused an Out-Of-Memory (OOM) error on the GPU, as SegFormer-B5 is very large and high-resolution images take up a lot of VRAM. Decision: Revert predict.py to process the original and flipped images sequentially to avoid OOM. Submit the final trained checkpoint (epoch_119.pt) for evaluation.
